title: Go 1.26 Toolchain, Module Graph, Dependencies, and Reproduction · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A preserved Go crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, transport, body, tokenizer, goquery, frontier, goroutine, Colly callback, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first broken invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in reproducible Go crawler repository baseline and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Pin Go 1.26.5, platform, architecture, module mode, build tags, toolchain directive, and supported runtime matrix.
Pin goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, and x/net 0.57.0 with compatibility, provenance, license, and maintenance evidence.
Bind source revision, module graph, generated fixtures, embed inputs, build settings, VCS metadata, binary digest, and configuration schema.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Version Contract
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 7175 during a malformed HTML and parser differential clinic. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile Go, pinned modules, build identity, clean gates, and baseline recovery; failed, canceled, stale, over-budget, and revoked work preserves prior admitted state; then change one tokenizer, goquery, selector, domain constructor, record, link, frontier, or trap case and retain rejected evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Pin Go 1.26.5, platform, architecture, module mode, build tags, toolchain directive, and supported runtime matrix.

Inspect the Version Contract trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Baseline Recovery
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-baseline-recovery-x1bz5x" that uses case 1195 to reconcile Go, pinned modules, build identity, clean gates, and baseline recovery. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
